title: Band Crossing
summary: A band crossing is a momentum-space point where two energy bands become degenerate; in Lecture One it is the local seed from which Weyl and Dirac low-energy descriptions are extracted.
assumptions: The crossing is analyzed in momentum space.
regime: Noninteracting or effectively single-particle band theory.
scope: Lecture One local crossing analysis in one, two, and three spatial dimensions.

source_anchors.notes: Generic two-band crossings are organized through H=a+b.sigma.

symmetries: Depends on symmetry class and dimension.
representation: Degenerate point in a Bloch Hamiltonian family.

math_expressions.label: localexp
math_expressions.context: Local two-band Hamiltonian whose degeneracy is controlled by the vanishing of the vector \vec b(p).

symbols.meaning: scalar energy shift in the local two-band Hamiltonian
Pauli-vector coefficient whose zero marks the crossing
Pauli matrices acting on the two-band subspace

failure_modes: Extended nodal lines or surfaces require different treatment.
Crossings away from the Fermi energy may not determine low-energy physics.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks what a Weyl or Dirac point is locally.
